headline: Treasury Yields Hit Session Highs After Fed Decision
summary: Treasury yields dipped before climbing to session highs after Federal Reserve officials held interest rates steady and signaled they expected to cut them one more time this year.  The yield on the 2-year Treasury, which often rises and falls with expectations for central-bank policy, was recently around 3.746%, according to Tradeweb, near their level prior to the Fed's announcement and Chairman Jerome Powell's news conference.

headline: Fed still expects to cut rates once this year despite spiking oil prices
summary: The central bank's so-called dot plot showed a median estimate of 3.4% for the federal funds rate, the same as at the end of last year.

headline: US FINANCIAL 15 SPLIT CORP. Preferred Dividend Declared
summary: TORONTO, March 18, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- US Financial 15 Split Corp ("US Financial 15") declares its monthly distribution of $0.06942 for each Preferred share, or 10.00% annually based on the previous month end net asset value. Distributions are payable April 10, 2026 to shareholders on record as at March 31, 2026. US Financial 15 invests in a portfolio primarily consisting of 15 U.S. financial services companies as follows: American Express, Bank of America, Bank of New York Mellon Corp., Ci

headline: CME Group (CME) Valuation Check After Recent Steady Share Price Momentum
summary: CME Group stock snapshot CME Group (CME) recently closed at $313.33, with total return figures of 3.4% over the past month and 14.5% over the past 3 months, putting current pricing in focus for investors. See our latest analysis for CME Group. The recent 14.5% 3 month share price return and 16.2% year to date share price return sit alongside a 22.1% 1 year total shareholder return, pointing to momentum that has been steady rather than explosive.
